title: Java Vector API
description: Practice the Java 26 Vector API incubator shape with FloatVector.
learningGoals: Use VectorSpecies and FloatVector to express lane-wise numeric operations.
Recognize that the Java 26 Vector API is an incubator module, enabled with --add-modules jdk.incubator.vector.

completionChecks.prompt: Which module must be added to compile this Vector API incubator lesson?
completionChecks.explanation: The Vector API lives in the jdk.incubator.vector module, so code using FloatVector needs that incubator module on the module graph.

choices: It computes the vector-width-safe upper bound before the scalar tail loop
It enables deep reflection over final fields
It removes the need for arrays
completionChecks.explanation: loopBound returns the largest index range that can be processed in complete vector chunks, leaving remaining elements for scalar handling.

choices: Adds two vectors lane by lane and stores the result chunk
Partitions stream values into Boolean keys
completionChecks.explanation: FloatVector operations work lane-wise. add combines corresponding lanes and intoArray writes that vector chunk back to the result array.
